sectionsUnavailable: Complete genome-wide inventory, coverage metrics, variant interpretation, pediatric context, disease risk, carrier status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, pathogenicity classification, clinical significance, and clinical actionability are unavailable for the local fixture.
Public Complete Genome Analysis inherited-traits sample rows are included only as observed output-structure evidence and are not local personal results.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A selected public BDNF rs6265 identifier is available for lookup and annotation-context validation, but no local inherited-trait, disease, medication, ancestry, pediatric, or clinical interpretation is supplied.
resultRows.sourceLabel: dbSNP / Ensembl / Sequence Ontology reference context
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This row demonstrates the local utility boundary: a public identifier can anchor lookup context, but it does not prove a complete genome-wide analysis or personal trait result.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The fixture supplies derived GRCh38 coordinate context while explicitly omitting alternate-build conversion, remapping evidence, complete callset output, and complete genome analysis service output.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Ensembl REST / NCBI Variation Services reference context
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Reference-build context can be reported, but it does not establish coverage, variant interpretation, pediatric interpretation, disease risk, carrier status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, or clinical significance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Complete genome-wide inventory, coverage metrics, variant interpretation, pediatric context, disease risk, carrier status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, pathogenicity classification, and clinical significance are unavailable in this fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Clinical and population genetics reference context
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local report can explain missing evidence and safety limits, but it must not create genome-wide, disease-risk, carrier-status, drug-response, ancestry, pediatric, pathogenicity, clinical, treatment, or all-clear claims.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample page is labeled Section 1 and INHERITED TRAITS, with a Complete Genome Science footer and page number 5.
resultRows.description: This captures visible section identity only; it does not show that a local complete genome analysis was run.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the single rendered page of the public sample PDF; section title and footer are observed output structure only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Physical Appearance as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local appearance finding.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the visible bullet list on the single rendered page of the public sample PDF; category only, no gene or result shown.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Nutrition and Diet Related as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local nutrition or diet-response finding.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the visible bullet list on the single rendered page of the public sample PDF; category only, no gene or result shown.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Blood Related as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local blood type or blood-trait finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Drug Response as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local medication-response or actionability finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Immune System as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local immune-status finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample lists Miscellaneous Traits as an inherited-traits category.
resultRows.description: This category label defines sample output organization and must not be treated as a local miscellaneous-trait finding.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the visible bullet list on the single rendered page of the public sample PDF; category only, no gene or result shown.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains inherited traits using hair color and blood type examples and says genes can interact with environment and lifestyle.
resultRows.description: This is sample explanatory framing only and does not support local appearance, blood type, lifestyle, or environmental interaction conclusions.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the explanatory paragraph below the category icons on the single rendered PDF page.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample shows possible result words including color labels, blood-type labels, likelihood labels, response labels, tolerance labels, direction labels, size labels, age labels, thickness labels, quantity labels, and wet/dry labels.
resultRows.description: These are example labels from the sample report and must not be used as local appearance, blood-type, drug-response, tolerance, age, size, or other trait calls.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the visible italic result-word list on the single rendered PDF page.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states that for the most part there is little a person can do to change inherited traits, while knowledge of underlying traits may inform appropriate lifestyle changes.
resultRows.description: This is sample limitations language only; local output must not turn it into personalized lifestyle, diet, medication, prevention, or treatment advice.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the final visible paragraph on the single rendered PDF page.

utilityContext.plainEnglishMeaning: This package is a local, reproducible Complete Genome Analysis boundary scaffold. It can show supplied utility context and observed inherited-traits sample-report structure, but it cannot replace complete genome-wide service output or qualified review.
unsupportedClaims: No claim that complete genome-wide inventory, coverage metrics, or variant interpretation was generated for the local fixture.

No local appearance, nutrition, blood type, drug-response, immune-system, miscellaneous-trait, disease-risk, carrier-status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, pediatric, pathogenicity, clinical-significance, treatment, lifestyle, or actionability claim.
No all-clear reassurance, diagnosis, screening recommendation, or treatment recommendation from the sample PDF or selected lookup rows.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: The local fixture supplies a public rsID row for lookup and annotation-context validation.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as bioinformatics utility context only; do not infer local complete genome analysis, inherited traits, disease risk, carrier status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: The local fixture intentionally omits complete genome analysis service output and clinical interpretation evidence.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Mark complete genome-wide, pediatric, disease-risk, carrier-status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, pathogenicity, clinical, treatment, and actionability sections unavailable instead of inferring them.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: The public sample PDF shows inherited-traits categories for Physical Appearance, Nutrition and Diet Related, Blood Related, Drug Response, Immune System, and Miscellaneous Traits.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample structure only; do not infer local appearance, nutrition, blood type, drug response, immune, or miscellaneous trait findings.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: The public sample PDF shows example result labels for colors, blood types, likelihood, resistance, tolerance, direction, size, age, thickness, quantity, and wet/dry traits.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample vocabulary only; do not convert example labels into local trait results or actionability.

missingInputs: Complete genome-wide inventory, coverage metrics, and variant interpretation output
Pediatric context, disease-risk model, carrier-status model, pharmacogenomics model, ancestry model, pathogenicity classification, and clinical-significance evidence
Local inherited-trait results for appearance, nutrition, blood type, drug response, immune status, or miscellaneous traits
Any clinical actionability, screening, treatment, lifestyle, medication, diet, or supplement guidance
uncertainty: No calibrated complete-genome, coverage, disease, carrier-status, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, pathogenicity, inherited-trait, or clinical-actionability model is supplied for this local package.
The sample PDF provides observed output structure only and cannot be treated as the user's personal result.
limitations: This validation result uses selected derived evidence only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, and complete callset files are not included.
The package does not emit complete SNP tables, sequencing files, alignment files, complete genome-wide analysis files, or clinical screening output files.

The package does not diagnose disease, estimate disease risk, classify pathogenicity, report carrier status, infer medication response, report ancestry, provide pediatric interpretation, or recommend screening, testing, treatment, diet, supplements, lifestyle, or medication changes.
